I can see the white "P" is now touching the green bar on the right side. The red blocks are visible at the overlap region.
Let me check the current state to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== Objects ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R', 'N', 'Y', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(r for r,c in b)
        maxr = max(r for r,c in b)
        minc = min(c for r,c in b)
        maxc = max(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({minr},{maxr},{minc},{maxc})")

# Check adjacency for white and green
print("\n=== Adjacency (W and N objects) ===")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = pair
    ci = next((n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == i), '?')
    cj = next((n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == j), '?')
    if ci in ['W', 'N'] or cj in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"{i}({ci}) - {j}({cj})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
